mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes C43 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned C43 AMG (M276.823) engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. The cost of a C43 AMG engine rebuild can vary widely, but typically ranges from £5,000 to £10,000 or more. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£15,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes C43 AMG engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M276 has a mixed reputation. It's a strong, smooth engine with impressive performance, but it has well-documented weaknesses: the timing chain tensioner oil feed issue causing cold-start rattle is the most common complaint. Misfire and cylinder head problems, HPFP failures, and oil leaks are also documented. With proper maintenance, these engines can be reliable, but the cold-start rattle is a genuine concern.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes C43 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Cold-start timing chain rattle — caused by secondary tensioner oil feed issues; a TSB (LI05.10-P-056435) exists for check valve and tensioner installation, (2) Misfire and cylinder head problems — causing severe shaking and loss of power under load, (3) High-pressure fuel pump failure — causing power loss and jerky running, and (4) Oil leaks from camshaft position sensors and timing cover.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes C43 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £15,000–£30,000 C43 AMG, a £5,000–£7,500 recon is 20–50% of the car's value. For a clean example with full service history, replacement is often worth it given the C43's strong retained values and enthusiast following. However, if the car has other issues (transmission, electrical faults, accident damage), evaluate total repair costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes C43 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The C43 AMG uses the M276.823 engine code — a 3.0-litre twin-turbo V6 producing 367–390 hp and 520 Nm of torque. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I fix the cold-start timing chain rattle?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the rattle is caused by secondary tensioner oil feed issues. Mercedes TSB LI05.10-P-056435 recommends installing check valves and chain tensioners. The cost is typically £700–£1,500. However, some dealers may not want to perform the work. The bulletin states no damage can be caused and it's just a noise, but many owners prefer to fix it for peace of mind.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes C43 AMG Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a C43 AMG engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The twin-turbo V6's complexity and the need for correct ECU calibration add labour time.

cta.label: → Compare C43 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Cold-Start Timing Chain Rattle (M276 Achilles Heel)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or ticking noise from the engine lasting 2–5 seconds on cold start, especially after the car has been sitting overnight; metallic noise that subsides as oil pressure builds
problems.typicalMileage: 30,000–120,000 miles — can occur at low mileage
problems.repairCost: £700–£1,500 (check valve and tensioner replacement) / £2,000–£4,000 (full timing chain kit)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M276 has a well-documented issue with secondary timing chain tensioner oil feed problems. When parked, oil drains back, causing a rattle for a few seconds until oil pressure returns. Mercedes has issued a Technical Service Bulletin (LI05.10-P-056435) recommending the installation of check valves and chain tensioners. Some dealers may not want to perform the work, and MB won't fix it for free either. The bulletin states no damage can be caused and it's just a noise — but many owners remain concerned. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, have the tensioners and check valves inspected. A £700–£1,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£11,000 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain tensioner guide → /failures/m276-timing-chain-rattle/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Misfire & Cylinder Head Issues
problems.symptoms: Engine shaking violently under acceleration, check engine light flashing, loss of power, multiple cylinder misfires (especially under load)
problems.typicalMileage: 4,000–40,000 miles — can occur on very low-mileage cars
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£4,000 (cylinder head inspection and repair) / £4,500–£7,500 (cylinder head replacement)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you experience severe shaking or misfires, stop driving and book an inspection

problems.recommendation: Multiple C43 AMG owners have reported severe engine misfires, particularly under hard acceleration. One owner reported that at just 4,160 miles, the car suffered from cylinders 2, 3, 4 and 6 misfiring after hard acceleration. The dealer had no solution, and the problem recurred repeatedly. Some owners have reported pistons going through the engine block, necessitating full engine replacement. If you experience rough running or misfires, have the cylinder head and fuel system inspected immediately. This is a serious issue that can lead to catastrophic engine failure.

failureLink.label: Read the full misfire & cylinder head guide → /failures/m276-misfire-cylinder-head/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) & Fuel System Issues
problems.symptoms: Loss of power under acceleration, jerky running when building boost, engine warning light, poor throttle response
problems.typicalMileage: 30,000–60,000 miles — can occur on tuned and stock cars

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you experience power loss or jerky running

problems.recommendation: The M276's high-pressure fuel pump and common rail system can suffer from corrosion and failure. One owner reported a severely corroded common rail, a broken LPFP, and a failing HPFP, with the car jerking when trying to build boost. If you experience power loss or jerky running under acceleration, have the fuel system inspected immediately. A £800–£2,000 repair is far cheaper than the engine damage that can follow if ignored.

failureLink.label: Read the full HPFP failure guide → /failures/hpfp-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Camshaft Position Sensors & Timing Cover)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell, visible oil residue around the camshaft position sensors or timing cover
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are a common issue on the M276 engine. Camshaft position sensors can leak oil into the wiring harness, causing electrical issues. Timing case cover and oil pan leaks are also documented. One MB shop foreman described a small leak near the cam magnet as "normal" but recommended replacing the gasket when time allows. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catching leaks early prevents oil contamination of other components and more serious engine damage.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: C43 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Check valve and tensioner replacement — bulletin fix available
Full engine replacement is the only reliable fix
Diagnosis required — may require head replacement
Catch early to prevent engine damage
Catch early to prevent electrical and engine damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This C43 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2018 facelift model with revised ECU calibration
* Full service history with regular oil changes using correct 5W-30 MB-spec oil
* No cold-start rattling — this is a critical warning sign
* Remainder of manufacturer warranty or extended warranty in place
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle with no documented tensioner/check valve work
* Check engine light with misfire codes — cylinder head issues may be present
* Oil leaks from camshaft position sensors or timing cover
* Poor service history — the M276 is a performance engine that needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes C43 AMG is a genuine performance saloon — 367–390 hp, 520 Nm, and 0-60 in 4.7 seconds. The M276 twin-turbo V6 is a strong, smooth engine with impressive performance, but it has well-documented weaknesses: timing chain tensioner oil feed issues causing cold-start rattle, cylinder head and misfire problems, HPFP failures, and oil leaks. The cold-start rattle is the most common complaint — a TSB exists but dealers may not want the work. Buy a well-maintained example with full service history and no rattling. Without history? Proceed with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare C43 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes C43 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking for the first 2–5 seconds (timing chain tensioner warning — this is critical)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for misfire or timing-related codes
* Service History — Including the M276.823 engine and confirmed oil change intervals (5W-30 MB-spec)
* Fuel System Health — Any power loss or jerky running under acceleration? (HPFP issues)
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the camshaft position sensors and timing cover
* Turbo Health — Listen for whistling; check for power loss (turbocharger wear)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
* Warranty Status — Extended warranty is highly recommended for these complex performance vehicles
